name: Debiasing Central Fixation Confounds Reveals a Peripheral "Sweet Spot" for Human-like Scanpaths in Hard-Attention Vision
summary: arXiv:2602.14834v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Human eye movements in visual recognition reflect a balance between foveal sampling and peripheral context. Task-driven hard-attention mode...
description: arXiv:2602.14834v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Human eye movements in visual recognition reflect a balance between foveal sampling and peripheral context. Task-driven hard-attention models for vision are often evaluated by how well their scanpaths match human gaze. However, common scanpath metrics can be strongly confounded by dataset-specific center bias, especially on object-centri...
author: Pengcheng Pan, Yonekura Shogo, Yasuo Kuniyosh
